name: Add a repository collaborator
description: Adds a github user as a repository collaborator, or updates their permission if already a collaborator; `permission` applies to organization-owned repositories (personal ones default to 'push' and ignore this field), and an invitation may be created or permissions updated directly.

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

username.title: Username
username.description: The GitHub handle for the user account to add as a collaborator.

permission.title: Permission
permission.description: Permission level for the collaborator. For organization-owned repositories, valid values are `pull` (read-only), `triage` (manage issues/PRs), `push` (read/write), `maintain` (manage most settings), `admin` (full control), or a custom repository role name if defined by the owning organization. This parameter is ignored for personal repositories, which grant `push` access.

successful.title: Successful

name: Compare two commits
description: Compares two commit points (commits, branches, tags, or shas) within a repository or across forks, using `base...head` or `owner:ref...owner:ref` format for the `basehead` parameter.

input_parameters.title: CompareTwoCommitsRequest

page.title: Page
page.description: Page number of the results to fetch, for pagination when the number of differences (commits or files) is large.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

basehead.title: Basehead
basehead.description: A string specifying the base and head references to compare, in the format `BASE...HEAD`. `BASE` and `HEAD` can be branch names (e.g., `main...develop`), tag names (e.g., `v1.0.0...v1.1.0`), or commit SHA-1s. Both references must exist in the specified repository. To compare branches across forks in the same network, use the format `OWNER:REF...OWNER:REF` (e.g., `octocat:main...another-user:main`).

per_page.title: Per Page
per_page.description: Number of results (e.g., files or commits) to return per page, with a maximum of 100, for pagination.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `CompareTwoCommits` action. Specifies the repository and the two commit points (base and head) to compare.

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary containing the comparison details. This typically includes: the status of the comparison (e.g., 'diverged', 'ahead', 'behind', 'identical'), the number of commits `ahead_by` and `behind_by` the base, `total_commits` in the range, an array of `commits` objects, an array of `files` objects detailing changed files (including status, additions, deletions, changes, patch, etc.), and various URLs (`html_url`, `permalink_url`, `diff_url`, `patch_url`) for accessing the comparison.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action compares two specific commits, branches, or tags in a repository. It provides detailed information on the differences between these two commit points, including the number of commits ahead or behind, and details about changed files.

human_description:
This action generates a Just-In-Time (JIT) configuration for a GitHub organization's new self-hosted runner. The generated configuration allows the runner to execute a single job and then unregister itself from GitHub Actions.

PREREQUISITES:
- You must have admin access to the GitHub organization
- The runner_group_id must already exist in the organization (you can list runner groups using GET /orgs/{org}/actions/runner-groups)
- Your authentication token needs the 'admin:org' scope

The encoded_jit_config returned expires after one hour and should be passed to the runner application to start it.

name: Create a check run
description: Creates a new check run for a specific commit in a repository, used by external services to report status, detailed feedback, annotations, and images directly within the github ui.

input_parameters.title: CreateACheckRunRequest

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the check to be displayed in the GitHub UI. For example, "Code Coverage" or "Linter".

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

status.title: Status
status.description: The current status of the check run. Only GitHub Actions can set `status` to `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested`.

actions.title: Actions
actions.description: A list of up to three action objects defining interactive buttons displayed on GitHub after the check run completes. Each object needs a `label` (button text), `identifier` (unique ID for the action), and `description` (tooltip). Clicking a button sends a `check_run.requested_action` webhook to your app.

head_sha.title: Head Sha
head_sha.description: The SHA of the commit for which the check run is created.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.description: **Required if `completed_at` is provided or if `status` is `completed`**. Specifies the final outcome of the check (e.g., 'success', 'failure'). Note: Setting `conclusion` automatically sets `status` to `completed`. GitHub alone can set a conclusion to `stale`.

started_at.title: Started At
started_at.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) indicating when the check run began.

details_url.title: Details Url
details_url.description: The URL of the integrator's site providing full details of the check. If omitted, the GitHub App's homepage is used. This URL is available from the check's UI.

external_id.title: External Id
external_id.description: A unique identifier for the check run on the integrator's system. This helps in correlating check runs between GitHub and the external system.

completed_at.title: Completed At
completed_at.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) indicating when the check run finished. **Required if `status` is `completed`**.

output__text.title: Output  Text
output__text.description: Detailed textual information about the check run's output. Supports Markdown. **Maximum length**: 65,535 characters.

output__title.title: Output  Title
output__title.description: A descriptive title for the output of the check run, displayed in the GitHub UI. For example, 'Linter Scan Summary'.

output__images.title: Output  Images
output__images.description: A list of image objects to display in the check run's output in the GitHub UI. Each object requires `alt` (alternative text), `image_url` (URL of the image), and an optional `caption`.

output__summary.title: Output  Summary
output__summary.description: A summary of the check run's findings. Supports Markdown. **Maximum length**: 65,535 characters.

output__annotations.title: Output  Annotations
output__annotations.description: A list of annotation objects detailing issues at specific code locations. Annotations are visible in the 'Checks' and 'Files changed' tabs of a pull request. Each annotation object should specify `path`, `start_line`, `end_line`, `annotation_level` ('notice', 'warning', or 'failure'), and `message`. Optional fields include `start_column`, `end_column`, `title`, and `raw_details`. Up to 50 annotations per API request; for more, use subsequent 'Update a check run' calls. GitHub Actions limits are 10 warnings and 10 errors per step.

input_parameters.description: Request schema to create a new check run on GitHub.

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary representing the full check run object as created by the API. This includes fields like `id`, `node_id`, `head_sha`, `status`, `conclusion`, `name`, `details_url`, `external_id`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, `output` (containing summary, text, annotations, images), `app` (information about the GitHub App that created the check run), and `pull_requests` (an array of pull requests associated with the commit).

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action creates a new check run for a specific commit in a repository. It allows external services to report their status, provide detailed feedback, and attach annotations and images directly within the GitHub interface.

**IMPORTANT: Authentication Requirement**
This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'checks:write' permission. OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (PAT) cannot create check runs. If you need to report status using a PAT, consider using the 'Create a commit status' action instead.

human_description: Creates a check suite for a specific commit in a repository. IMPORTANT: This action requires a GitHub App installation access token with 'checks:write' permission. OAuth tokens and classic personal access tokens CANNOT use this endpoint (will return 404). By default, check suites are automatically created when check runs are created - only use this to manually create check suites when automatic creation has been disabled.

name: Create an organization webhook
description: Creates a webhook for a github organization to deliver event notifications to a configured url.

input_parameters.title: CreateAnOrganizationWebhookRequest

org.title: Org
org.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the webhook. Must be "web" for organization webhooks.

active.title: Active
active.description: Boolean indicating if the webhook is active. `true` sends notifications, `false` does not. Defaults to `true`.

events.title: Events
events.description: List of event names to trigger the webhook. Use `['*']` for all events. Defaults to `['push']` if not specified. See GitHub docs for event types.

config__url.title: Config  Url
config__url.description: The target URL for webhook payload delivery. This URL is a mandatory part of the webhook's `config` object and will receive POST requests for subscribed events.

config__secret.title: Config  Secret
config__secret.description: Optional secret for generating an HMAC hex digest for payload verification (X-Hub-Signature-256 header). If provided, this is used as the key.

config__password.title: Config  Password
config__password.description: Password for basic authentication if the webhook endpoint (`config_url`) requires it, corresponding to `config_username`.

config__username.title: Config  Username
config__username.description: Username for basic authentication if the webhook endpoint (`config_url`) requires it. Use only if your endpoint is protected by basic auth.

config__content__type.title: Config  Content  Type
config__content__type.description: The media type for serializing webhook payloads. Supported: `json`, `form`. GitHub API defaults to `form` if this field is not set or is `None`.

config__insecure__ssl.title: Config  Insecure  Ssl
config__insecure__ssl.description: Controls SSL certificate verification for `config_url`. '1' disables verification (not recommended), '0' enables. GitHub API defaults to '0' (verification enabled) if this field is not set or is `None`.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `CreateAnOrganizationWebhook`

data.title: Data

name: Create a pull request
description: Creates a pull request in a github repository, requiring existing `base` and `head` branches; `title` or `issue` must be provided.

input_parameters.title: CreateAPullRequestRequest

base.title: Base
base.description: The name of the branch you want the changes pulled into. This must be an existing branch on the current (target) repository. You cannot submit a pull request to one repository that requests a merge to a base branch of another repository.

body.title: Body
examples: This PR introduces a new feature that does X, Y, and Z.
Fixes #42 by addressing the off-by-one error.
body.description: The detailed description or contents of the pull request.

head.title: Head
head.description: The name of the branch where your changes are implemented. For cross-repository pull requests, namespace `head` with the source owner and branch, like `username:branch`.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

draft.title: Draft
draft.description: Indicates whether the pull request should be created as a draft. Draft pull requests cannot be merged until marked as ready for review.

issue.title: Issue
issue.description: The number of an existing issue in the repository to convert into a pull request. If provided, the issue's title and body may be used for the pull request. Required if `title` is not specified.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

title.title: Title
title.description: The title of the new pull request. Required unless `issue` is specified.

head_repo.title: Head Repo
head_repo.description: The name of the repository (e.g., 'octocat/Hello-World') where the changes in the pull request were made. This field is required for cross-repository pull requests if both the source and target repositories are owned by the same organization but are different repositories.

maintainer_can_modify.title: Maintainer Can Modify
maintainer_can_modify.description: Indicates whether maintainers of the upstream repository can modify the pull request. This is primarily relevant for pull requests originating from forks.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `CreateAPullRequest`

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary containing the full representation of the created pull request.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a pull request in a specified repository. It requires the base and head branches to exist and necessitates either a title or an issue to be provided.

name: Create a reference
description: Creates a git reference (e.g., a branch or tag) in a repository; the repository must not be empty prior to this operation.

input_parameters.title: CreateAReferenceRequest

ref.title: Ref
ref.description: Fully qualified reference to create (e.g., `refs/heads/master`, `refs/tags/v1.0.0`). Must start with `refs/` and contain at least two slashes.

sha.title: Sha
sha.description: SHA-1 hash of an existing commit in the repository that the new reference will point to.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `CreateAReference`

data.title: Data
data.description: Details of the newly created Git reference, typically including its `ref`, `node_id`, `url`, and target object (`sha`, `type`).

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new Git reference such as a branch or tag within a specified repository. It requires the owner and repository name, along with a reference and the SHA of the commit it points to. IMPORTANT: This action can only create NEW references. If the reference already exists, the API will return a 422 error with 'Reference already exists'. To modify an existing branch or tag, use the 'Update a reference' action (GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REFERENCE) instead. To check if a reference exists before creating it, use 'Get a reference' (GITHUB_GET_A_REFERENCE) or 'List matching references' (GITHUB_LIST_MATCHING_REFERENCES).

name: Create a release
description: Creates a release in a github repository for a specified tag; the tag must be unique for published releases, and if a `discussion category name` is given, it must already exist.

body.title: Body
examples: This release includes new features X, Y, and bug fixes for Z.

# New Features
- Feature A
- Feature B
body.description: A detailed description of the release. Markdown formatting is supported.

name.title: Name
name.description: The title of the release. If omitted and `generate_release_notes` is false, it defaults to the `tag_name`.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the .git extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

draft.title: Draft
draft.description: Set to `true` to create an unpublished (draft) release. Defaults to `false`.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name). This field is not case-sensitive.

tag_name.title: Tag Name
tag_name.description: The name of the tag for this release. This tag must be unique for published releases.

prerelease.title: Prerelease
prerelease.description: Set to `true` to identify this release as a pre-release. Defaults to `false`.

make_latest.title: Make Latest
make_latest.description: Specifies if this release should be marked as the latest for the repository. Valid values: 'true', 'false', 'legacy'. Defaults to 'true'. Draft releases and prereleases cannot be set as 'latest'.

target_commitish.title: Target Commitish
target_commitish.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) from which the Git tag is created. Defaults to the repository's default branch if the tag specified in `tag_name` doesn't exist yet.

generate_release_notes.title: Generate Release Notes
generate_release_notes.description: Set to `true` to automatically generate the release title (`name`) and description (`body`) from the commit history since the last release. Defaults to `false`.

discussion_category_name.title: Discussion Category Name
discussion_category_name.description: If specified, a new discussion will be created in this category and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository's discussions.

id.title: Id

comments.description: Inline draft review comments. Each object requires `path` (relative file path) and `body` (comment text). Example: `[{'path': 'file.py', 'line': 10, 'body': 'Refactor this.'}]`

name: Create a dependency snapshot
description: Creates a snapshot of a repository's dependencies to populate the github dependency graph and enable security alerts; `sha` must be a 40-character commit id, `ref` a fully qualified git reference (e.g., `refs/heads/main`), and `scanned` an iso 8601 timestamp.

input_parameters.title: CreateASnapshotOfDependenciesForARepositoryRequest

ref.title: Ref
ref.description: Git reference (fully qualified, e.g., `refs/heads/main`) of the repository branch/tag that triggered this snapshot.

sha.title: Sha
sha.description: Commit SHA (maximum 40 characters) associated with this dependency snapshot.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: Repository name, without `.git` extension (name is not case-sensitive).

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: Account owner of the repository (name is not case-sensitive).

job__id.title: Job  Id
job__id.description: Optional external ID of the job that generated this snapshot.

scanned.title: Scanned
scanned.description: UTC timestamp (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) when the snapshot was taken.

version.title: Version
version.description: Version of the dependency snapshot submission, typically of the manifest or lock file format.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Optional user-defined metadata for domain-specific information. Limited to 8 keys with scalar values (e.g., string, number, boolean).

manifests.title: Manifests
manifests.description: Optional collection of package manifests (e.g., package-lock.json, pom.xml). Keys are manifest file names; values detail dependencies.

detector__url.title: Detector  Url
detector__url.description: Optional URL of the detector tool used.

detector__name.title: Detector  Name
detector__name.description: Optional name of the tool used to detect dependencies.

job__html__url.title: Job  Html  Url
job__html__url.description: Optional URL for the job that generated this snapshot.

job__correlator.title: Job  Correlator
job__correlator.description: Optional string to group snapshots. For a given `job.correlator` and `detector.name`, only the 'latest' snapshot is current. Useful to distinguish detection runs (e.g., concatenating GITHUB_WORKFLOW and GITHUB_JOB).

detector__version.title: Detector  Version
detector__version.description: Optional version of the detector tool used.

input_parameters.description: Input for creating a dependency snapshot, capturing the repository's dependency state at a specific commit.

data.title: Data
data.description: ID of the created snapshot and a confirmation message.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a snapshot of a repository's dependencies at a specified commit. It captures the current state of the repository's dependencies and helps in managing security alerts and insights on GitHub.

name: Create a tree
description: Creates a new git tree object in a repository, defining file/directory structure by specifying tree entries, optionally building on a `base tree` sha; all provided shas must be valid.

input_parameters.title: CreateATreeRequest

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

tree.title: Tree
tree.description: List of objects defining the tree structure. Each object (node) requires `path` (string), `mode` (string; e.g., '100644' file, '100755' executable, '040000' subdirectory/tree, '160000' submodule, '120000' symlink), and `type` (string: 'blob', 'tree', or 'commit'). For new/updated blobs, provide `content` (UTF-8 string). For existing objects or `tree`/`commit` types, provide `sha` (string). To delete, specify `path`, `mode`, `type`, and set `sha` to `null`.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

base_tree.title: Base Tree
base_tree.description: SHA1 of an existing Git tree object to use as a base. If provided, its entries are combined with `tree` entries, where `tree` entries overwrite those from `base_tree` with the same path. Typically the SHA1 of the latest commit's tree for new branch changes. If omitted, the new tree is built from `tree` entries; files from the parent commit's tree not in `tree` are treated as deleted.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `CreateATree`

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary representing the created Git tree object. It includes the `sha` and `url` of the new tree, an array of `tree` items (nodes in the tree), and a `truncated` boolean flag indicating if the number of items in the tree array exceeded the maximum limit and was cut short.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Creates a new Git tree object in a repository by defining the structure of files and directories. This action allows users to specify tree entries, optionally building on a provided base tree SHA for more complex tree structures.

IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires OAuth App owner credentials (client_id/client_secret) for Basic Authentication, not a personal access token. Use this when you need to completely revoke an application's access to a user's account, such as when a user uninstalls your app or when handling security incidents.

successful.title: Successful

human_description: Revokes a single OAuth access token for your OAuth App or GitHub App. This immediately invalidates the specific token provided, revoking all API access for that token. CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS: (1) You must be the OAuth App owner with access to both client_id AND client_secret, (2) This endpoint requires Basic Authentication (client_id as username, client_secret as password), (3) The access_token must have been issued by YOUR OAuth App matching the client_id, (4) This endpoint CANNOT be called with regular user tokens or PATs. Use this when you need to revoke a specific user token, such as when a user signs out or for security incident response.

configuration_file_path.title: Configuration File Path
configuration_file_path.description: Specifies a path to a configuration file in the repository (e.g., `.github/release.yml`) containing settings for generating release notes. If unspecified, GitHub looks for a configuration file at ".github/release.yml" or ".github/release.yaml". If no such file is found, default settings are used.

## Changes

name: Get a reference
description: Retrieves a specific git reference (e.g., a branch, tag, or fully qualified like 'heads/main') from a github repository.

input_parameters.title: GetAReferenceRequest

ref.title: Ref
ref.description: The Git reference to retrieve. This can be a branch name (e.g., `main`), a tag name (e.g., `v1.0.0`), or a fully qualified reference (e.g., `heads/main`, `tags/v1.0.0`). For more information, see "[Git References](https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Internals-Git-References)" in the Git documentation.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `GetAReference`

data.title: Data
data.description: Details of the Git reference: its `ref` string, `node_id`, API `url`, and the referenced `object` (e.g., commit, tag) specifying its `type`, `sha` (SHA-1 hash), and object API `url`.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action retrieves a specific Git reference from a GitHub repository.
It allows users to access information about branches, tags, or other references in the specified repository.
IMPORTANT: The reference must actually exist in the repository. If you receive a 404 error, the branch or tag
does not exist. Consider using 'list_matching_references' action first to discover available references if you're
unsure. Note that repositories may use 'main' instead of 'master' as the default branch.

name: Get a thread subscription for the authenticated user
description: Retrieves the authenticated user's subscription details for a specific notification thread, identified by `thread id`.

input_parameters.title: GetAThreadSubscriptionForTheAuthenticatedUserRequest

thread_id.title: Thread Id
thread_id.description: The unique identifier of the notification thread. This ID corresponds to the `id` field returned when retrieving notifications, for example, through the 'List notifications for the authenticated user' (GET /notifications) operation.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `GetAThreadSubscriptionForTheAuthenticatedUser`

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary representing the thread subscription object. Key fields typically include `subscribed` (boolean: whether the user is receiving notifications for this thread), `ignored` (boolean: whether the user has muted this thread), `reason` (nullable string: the reason for the current subscription status), `created_at` (ISO 8601 timestamp: when the subscription was created), `url` (API URL for this subscription), and `thread_url` (API URL for the parent thread).

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Retrieves the subscription details for a specific notification thread for the authenticated user. This action allows the user to determine their current subscription status for the thread identified by `thread_id`.

Use recursive mode to fetch the entire repository tree (all files and folders) in one efficient call, ideal for browsing repository contents, analyzing project structure, or locating specific files. Returns file paths, types, sizes, and git blob references for all items.

repository_query.description: A query string to filter repositories within the organization. For detailed syntax, consult GitHub's documentation on constructing search queries and searching for repositories.

data.description: The response from the API, typically a list of repository objects. Note: The API returns a list of objects, but the type hint is `t.Dict[str, t.Any]`; the data should be processed as a list of dictionaries where each dictionary represents a repository.

name: List jobs for a workflow run
description: Lists jobs for a specific workflow run in a github repository.

input_parameters.title: ListJobsForAWorkflowRunRequest

page.title: Page
page.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more details, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the .git extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

filter.title: Filter
filter.description: Filters jobs by their `completed_at` timestamp. `latest` returns jobs from the most recent execution attempt of the workflow run. `all` returns all jobs for a workflow run, including from previous execution attempts.

run_id.title: Run Id
run_id.description: The unique identifier of the workflow run for which jobs are to be listed.

per_page.title: Per Page
per_page.description: The number of results to return per page, with a maximum of 100. For more details, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

input_parameters.description: Request schema for listing jobs associated with a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository.

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary containing the list of jobs and related metadata. Each job typically includes details like 'id', 'name', 'status' (e.g., 'queued', 'in_progress', 'completed'), 'conclusion' (e.g., 'success', 'failure', 'skipped'), 'started_at', and 'completed_at' timestamps. For a detailed structure, refer to the official GitHub API documentation for workflow jobs.

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
Lists pull requests for a GitHub repository.

**REQUIRED PARAMETERS (MUST BE PROVIDED):**
- owner: The GitHub username or organization (e.g., 'octocat', 'microsoft')
- repo: The repository name (e.g., 'hello-world', 'vscode')

Both 'owner' and 'repo' are MANDATORY. The action will fail if either is missing.

OPTIONAL filters: state (open/closed/all), head branch, base branch, and sorting options.

NOTE: This action does NOT support 'assignee' or 'since' parameters. To search for pull requests by assignee, author, or across multiple repositories, use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead.

ref.title: Ref

after.title: After

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This name is not case-sensitive.

before.title: Before
before.description: A pagination cursor. Returns activity items that occurred before this cursor. This cursor is typically obtained from the `Link` header in a previous API response. For more details, see GitHub's REST API documentation on "Using pagination in the REST API" and "Using Link headers".

per_page.title: Per Page
per_page.description: The number of activity results to return per page. Maximum value is 100. For more information, see the GitHub REST API guide on "Using pagination in the REST API".

direction.title: Direction
direction.description: The direction to sort the activity results. Use 'asc' for ascending (oldest first) or 'desc' for descending (newest first).

time_period.title: Time Period
time_period.description: Filters activities based on a predefined time period. For example, 'day' retrieves activities from the last 24 hours, and 'week' from the last 7 days (168 hours).

activity_type.title: Activity Type
activity_type.description: Filters activities by a specific type, such as 'push' or 'pr_merge'. If unspecified, all relevant activity types are returned.

name: Merge a pull request
description: Merges an open and mergeable pull request in a repository, optionally specifying merge commit details, a merge method, and a required head sha for safety.

input_parameters.title: MergeAPullRequestRequest

sha.description: SHA of the pull request's head commit. Must match for merge to succeed, ensuring PR hasn't changed since review.

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action merges an open pull request in a repository. You can specify details like the merge method and commit message.

**IMPORTANT: A 405 error can occur for multiple reasons:**
- **Draft PRs cannot be merged.** If the pull request is still in draft mode, you must first mark it as ready for review before merging. Use the GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST action or the GitHub web UI to convert the draft to a regular PR.
- **Branch protection rules not satisfied.** The repository may have branch protection rules requiring approving reviews, passing status checks, or other conditions. Check the error message for details like "At least 1 approving review is required" or "Required status checks have not passed".
- **Base branch was modified.** If the base branch has been updated since the PR was last synced, you must update the PR branch first. Use the GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST_BRANCH action to sync the PR with the latest base branch changes, then retry the merge.

start_side.title: Start Side

in_reply_to.title: In Reply To
in_reply_to.description: ID of an existing review comment to reply to. If provided, other location parameters (except `body`) are ignored.

pull_number.title: Pull Number
pull_number.description: Identifier of the pull request.

subject_type.title: Subject Type
subject_type.description: Level of the comment target: `line` (specific line) or `file` (entire file). Defaults to `line` if not provided.

squash_merge_commit_message.description: Default message for squash merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'COMMIT_MESSAGES', or 'BLANK'.

sha.title: Sha
sha.description: The blob SHA of the file being replaced. Required to update an existing file; omit to create a new file.

owner.description: The account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

message.title: Message
message.description: The commit message for this file creation or update.

author__date.title: Author  Date
author__date.description: Author's timestamp (ISO 8601 format). Defaults to committer's date if author details are provided but date is not.

author__name.title: Author  Name
author__name.description: Author's name. If specified, `author_email` is also required. Defaults to committer details; if all committer/author details omitted, GitHub uses authenticated user.

author__email.title: Author  Email
author__email.description: Author's email. If specified, `author_name` is also required. Defaults to committer details; if all committer/author details omitted, GitHub uses authenticated user.

committer__date.title: Committer  Date
committer__date.description: Committer's commit timestamp (ISO 8601 format). Defaults to current time if committer details are provided but date is not.

committer__name.title: Committer  Name
committer__name.description: Committer's name. If specified, `committer_email` is also required. GitHub uses authenticated user if all committer/author details omitted.

committer__email.title: Committer  Email
committer__email.description: Committer's email. If specified, `committer_name` is also required. GitHub uses authenticated user if all committer/author details omitted.

description: Deprecated: gets repository file content or directory metadata; use `get repository content` instead.

input_parameters.title: GetRepositoryContentRequest

ref.title: Ref
ref.description: The name of the commit, branch, or tag. If not provided, the repository's default branch will be used.

path.title: Path
path.description: The path to the content in the repository. This can be a file or a directory path.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for retrieving content from a GitHub repository.

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary representing the file content or directory metadata. If `path` points to a file, this dictionary includes keys such as `name`, `path`, `sha`, `size`, `type` ('file'), `content` (Base64 encoded string of the file's contents), and `download_url`. If `path` points to a directory, this dictionary represents metadata for the directory object itself (e.g., its `name`, `path`, `sha`, `type` ('dir')). Note: To list the actual items *within* a directory, a different API endpoint or action might be needed, as the GitHub API returns an array of items for directory listings.

name: Rerequest a check run
description: Triggers a re-run of a specific check run in a github repository, which resets its status to 'queued', clears its conclusion, and triggers the `check run` webhook with `rerequested`.

input_parameters.title: RerequestACheckRunRequest

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is case-insensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name). This field is case-insensitive.

check_run_id.title: Check Run Id
check_run_id.description: The unique numerical identifier of the check run to be re-requested.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for re-requesting a check run. Specifies the repository and the check run to be re-triggered.

data.title: Data
data.description: An empty dictionary indicating a successful rerequest. The HTTP status code will be 201 Created upon success.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action re-triggers a specific check run in a repository, resetting its status.

**IMPORTANT: Authentication Requirement**
This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'checks:write' permission. The check run can only be re-requested by the same GitHub App that originally created it. OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (PAT) cannot use this endpoint.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `RerequestACheckSuite`

data.title: Data
data.description: The response body from the GitHub API. Typically, this is an empty object for a successful request, as the main outcome is the rerequest of the check suite.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
Triggers a new run of an existing check suite within a repository, resetting its status.

**IMPORTANT: Authentication Requirement**
This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'checks:write' permission. OAuth tokens and classic personal access tokens (PAT) cannot use this endpoint. Only GitHub App user access tokens, GitHub App installation access tokens, or fine-grained personal access tokens with checks:write permission are supported.

This action is useful for re-running checks without new code when a previous check suite did not pass or needs to be retried.

human_description:
This action revokes the installation access token for a GitHub App. Once revoked, the token can no longer be used for API authentication.

IMPORTANT REQUIREMENTS:
- Requires a GitHub App installation access token (ghs_* prefix) - OAuth tokens will receive 403 Forbidden
- This is a destructive, irreversible action - the revoked token cannot be restored
- After revocation, a new token must be generated to continue API operations
- Returns HTTP 204 No Content on success (empty response body)

Specifies the level of access workflows outside this repository have to its actions and reusable workflows.
- `none`: Actions and reusable workflows are accessible only from workflows within this repository.
- `user`: Actions and reusable workflows can be shared with other private repositories owned by the same user.
- `organization`: Actions and reusable workflows can be shared with other repositories within the same organization.

name: Update a check run
description: Updates an existing check run for a specific commit in a repository, allowing modifications to its status, conclusion, output, and other details.

input_parameters.title: UpdateACheckRunRequest

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the check (e.g., "code-coverage").

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: Repository name without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

status.title: Status
status.description: Current status of the check run. Only GitHub Actions can set `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested`.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.description: Final conclusion of the check. Required if `completed_at` is provided or `status` is `completed`. Providing `conclusion` sets `status` to `completed`. Users cannot set `stale`.

started_at.title: Started At
started_at.description: Timestamp of when the check run started (ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

details_url.title: Details Url
details_url.description: URL of the integrator's site with full check details.

external_id.title: External Id
external_id.description: Reference for the run on the integrator's system.

check_run_id.title: Check Run Id
check_run_id.description: The unique identifier of the check run.

completed_at.title: Completed At
completed_at.description: Timestamp of when the check run completed (ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

output__text.title: Output  Text
output__text.description: Text for the output, can contain Markdown.

output__images.title: Output  Images
output__images.description: Images for the output, displayed in the GitHub PR UI.

output__summary.title: Output  Summary
output__summary.description: Summary of the output, can contain Markdown. Required if the `output` object is provided.

output__annotations.title: Output  Annotations
output__annotations.description: Adds line-specific analysis information, visible in PRs. Max 50 per request (appended to existing). GitHub Actions: 10 warnings/10 errors per step.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `UpdateACheckRun`

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action updates an existing check run associated with a commit in a repository. It can modify details like the check's status, conclusion, and output, helping integrate checks smoothly into the development process.

**IMPORTANT: Authentication Requirement**
This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'checks:write' permission. OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (PAT) cannot update check runs. Only the GitHub App that created the check run can update it.

For more information about pattern matching syntax, refer to the Ruby File.fnmatch documentation (https://ruby-doc.org/core-2.5.1/File.html#method-c-fnmatch).

org.title: Org
org.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

value.title: Value

visibility.title: Visibility
visibility.description: Specifies the new visibility of the organization variable. It determines which repositories in the organization can access this variable. `selected` visibility requires `selected_repository_ids` to be populated. If omitted, visibility is not changed.

selected_repository_ids.title: Selected Repository Ids
selected_repository_ids.description: A list of repository integer IDs that should have access to the organization variable. This field is required and only used when `visibility` is set to `selected`. If omitted, the selection of repositories is not changed (unless `visibility` itself is changed away from `selected`).

name: Update an organization webhook
description: Updates the configuration (url, content type, secret, ssl verification), subscribed events, active status, or name of an existing webhook for a specified organization.

input_parameters.title: UpdateAnOrganizationWebhookRequest

org.title: Org
org.description: The name of the organization. This value is not case-sensitive.

name.title: Name

active.title: Active
active.description: Set to `true` to send notifications, or `false` to disable them.

events.title: Events
events.description: List of events that trigger this webhook. Use `['*']` to subscribe to all events. See GitHub docs for available event types.

hook_id.title: Hook Id
hook_id.description: The unique identifier of the webhook. This ID can be found in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery.

config__url.title: Config  Url
config__url.description: Target URL for webhook payloads. Required if updating other `config` properties (e.g., `content_type`, `secret`), as GitHub mandates `url` when `config` is modified.

config__secret.title: Config  Secret
config__secret.description: Secret key for HMAC digest to ensure payload integrity. Refer to GitHub documentation on delivery headers for details.

config__content__type.title: Config  Content  Type
config__content__type.description: Media type for serializing payloads (`json` or `form`). The API defaults to `form` if this field is not provided.

config__insecure__ssl.title: Config  Insecure  Ssl
config__insecure__ssl.description: Controls SSL certificate verification for payload delivery. Set to '1' to skip verification (insecure), or '0' to enforce (API default). Use with caution.

input_parameters.description: Request payload for updating an organization webhook. Only include fields that need to be modified.

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

repo.description: Repository name, excluding `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

force.title: Force
force.description: If `true`, forces the update even if not a fast-forward. If `false`, only fast-forward updates are allowed, preventing data overwrites.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `UpdateAReference`

data.title: Data
data.description: Details of the updated or created Git reference, including its `ref` name, `node_id`, API `url`, and commit object information.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates an existing Git reference to point to a given commit SHA. By default, only fast-forward updates are allowed to prevent accidental history rewriting. Use `force=true` when you need to move a branch backward or rewrite history. The reference must already exist - use the 'Create a reference' action to create new refs.

name: Update a release
description: Updates an existing release in a github repository, allowing modification of its attributes; if linking a discussion, the `discussion category name` must refer to an existing category in the repository.

input_parameters.title: UpdateAReleaseRequest

body.title: Body
examples: This release includes performance improvements and bug fixes related to user authentication.
body.description: Text describing the contents of the release. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown.

name.title: Name
name.description: The title of the release.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

draft.title: Draft
draft.description: Set to `true` to mark the release as a draft, or `false` to publish it. Default: `false`.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

tag_name.title: Tag Name
tag_name.description: The name of the tag. This can be an existing tag or a new tag name.

prerelease.title: Prerelease
prerelease.description: Set to `true` to identify the release as a pre-release, or `false` for a full release. Default: `false`.

release_id.title: Release Id
release_id.description: The unique identifier of the release to be updated.

make_latest.title: Make Latest
make_latest.description: Specifies whether this release should be set as the latest release for the repository. Accepts 'true', 'false', or 'legacy'. Drafts and pre-releases cannot be set as latest. Defaults to `true` for newly published releases. `legacy` specifies that the latest release should be determined based on the release creation date and higher semantic version.

target_commitish.title: Target Commitish
target_commitish.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name or commit SHA) that determines where the Git tag is created from. Can be any branch or commit SHA. This parameter is unused if the Git tag specified by `tag_name` already exists. Defaults to the repository's default branch if omitted.

discussion_category_name.title: Discussion Category Name
discussion_category_name.description: If specified, a discussion of this category is created and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository. This parameter is ignored if a discussion is already linked to the release. For more information, see "[Managing categories for discussions in your repository](https://docs.github.com/discussions/managing-discussions-for-your-community/managing-categories-for-discussions-in-your-repository)."

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `UpdateARelease`

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates the attributes of an existing release in a GitHub repository. Users can modify information such as the release title, description, tag name, and more, allowing for better management of releases within their repositories.

input_parameters.title: UpdateAReleaseAssetRequest

name.title: Name
name.description: The new file name for the asset. This updates the display name of the asset.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

label.title: Label
label.description: An alternate short description for the asset. This is used in place of the filename if provided, offering a more descriptive label.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

state.title: State
state.description: The new state of the release asset. While the GitHub API documentation primarily shows 'state' in responses (e.g., 'uploaded'), this field allows attempting to update it. The effect and accepted values might be specific.

asset_id.title: Asset Id
asset_id.description: The unique identifier of the release asset to be updated.

input_parameters.description: Parameters to identify and update a specific release asset.

data.title: Data
data.description: Comprehensive details of the updated release asset, reflecting applied changes.

error.title: Error

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action allows users to update essential properties of a release asset 
    such as its name, label, or current state within a GitHub repository. It ensures that the release asset 
    reflects the most accurate information based on user specifications.

name: Update a repository
description: Updates settings for an existing github repository, such as name, description, visibility, merge strategies, and security configurations.

input_parameters.title: UpdateARepositoryRequest

name.title: Name
name.description: New name for the repository (not case-sensitive).

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: Username or organization name of the repository owner (not case-sensitive).

private.title: Private
private.description: Whether the repository is private. Note: Changing visibility might be restricted by organization policies, potentially causing a 422 error.

archived.title: Archived
archived.description: Archives the repository (making it read-only) or unarchives it.

has_issues.title: Has Issues
has_issues.description: Enables GitHub Issues for this repository.

description.title: Description
examples: A library for advanced data processing.
description.description: Brief summary of the repository.

is_template.title: Is Template
is_template.description: Designates this repository as a template repository.

has_projects.title: Has Projects
has_projects.description: Enables GitHub Projects. Note: Organization settings might override this and cause an error if projects are disabled organization-wide.

allow_forking.title: Allow Forking
allow_forking.description: Allows private forks of this repository.

allow_merge_commit.title: Allow Merge Commit
allow_merge_commit.description: Allows merging pull requests via merge commits.

merge_commit_title.title: Merge Commit Title
merge_commit_title.description: Default title for merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'MERGE_MESSAGE'.

allow_update_branch.title: Allow Update Branch
allow_update_branch.description: Allows updating a pull request's head branch from the base branch if not strictly required for merging.

merge_commit_message.title: Merge Commit Message
merge_commit_message.description: Default message for merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'PR_TITLE', or 'BLANK'.

delete_branch_on_merge.title: Delete Branch On Merge
delete_branch_on_merge.description: Automatically deletes head branches after merging pull requests.

squash_merge_commit_title.title: Squash Merge Commit Title
squash_merge_commit_title.description: Default title for squash merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE'.

squash_merge_commit_message.title: Squash Merge Commit Message
squash_merge_commit_message.description: Default message for squash merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'COMMIT_MESSAGES', or 'BLANK'.

web_commit_signoff_required.title: Web Commit Signoff Required
web_commit_signoff_required.description: Requires contributors to sign off on web-based commits.

use_squash_pr_title_as_default.title: Use Squash Pr Title As Default
use_squash_pr_title_as_default.description: DEPRECATED: Use `squash_merge_commit_title`. If true, squash merge commits use PR title; otherwise, commit message.

security__and__analysis__secret__scannin.title: Security  And  Analysis  Secret  Scanning  Status
security__and__analysis__secret__scannin.description: Manages secret scanning. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

security__and__analysis__advanced__secur.title: Security  And  Analysis  Advanced  Security  Status
security__and__analysis__advanced__secur.description: Manages GitHub Advanced Security. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

security__and__analysis__secret__scannin.title: Security  And  Analysis  Secret  Scanning  Push  Protection  Status
security__and__analysis__secret__scannin.description: Manages secret scanning push protection. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

name: Update a repository ruleset
description: Updates an existing repository ruleset, identified by `ruleset id` for a given repository, allowing partial updates to its configuration such as name, target, enforcement, bypass actors, conditions, and rules.

input_parameters.title: UpdateARepositoryRulesetRequest

name.title: Name
name.description: The new name for the ruleset.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

rules.title: Rules
rules.description: Rules to enforce. Each rule has a `type` and optional `parameters` (e.g., `{'type': 'creation'}` or `{'type': 'pull_request', 'parameters': {'required_approving_review_count': 2}}`). Refer to GitHub API documentation for available rule types and their parameters.

target.title: Target
target.description: The target of the ruleset (e.g., branch or tag).

ruleset_id.title: Ruleset Id
ruleset_id.description: The unique identifier of the existing ruleset to be updated.

enforcement.title: Enforcement
enforcement.description: Specifies the enforcement level: `disabled` (ruleset is ignored), `active` (ruleset is enforced), or `evaluate` (allows testing rules before enforcement - GitHub Enterprise only).

bypass_actors.title: Bypass Actors
bypass_actors.description: Actors that can bypass ruleset. Each actor specifies `actor_id`, `actor_type` (`RepositoryRole`, `Team`, `Integration`, `OrganizationAdmin`), and `bypass_mode` (`always`, `pull_request`). Example: `[{'actor_id': 1, 'actor_type': 'RepositoryRole', 'bypass_mode': 'always'}]`.

conditions__ref__name__exclude.title: Conditions  Ref  Name  Exclude
conditions__ref__name__exclude.description: Ref names or patterns (fnmatch-style globs) to exclude. The ruleset will not apply if any of these patterns match.

conditions__ref__name__include.title: Conditions  Ref  Name  Include
conditions__ref__name__include.description: Ref names or patterns (fnmatch-style globs) to include for the ruleset to apply. `~DEFAULT_BRANCH` targets the default branch; `~ALL` targets all branches.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `UpdateARepositoryRuleset`

data.title: Data
data.description: The updated repository ruleset object.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates an existing repository ruleset identified by ruleset_id. It allows for modifications to various configuration options including the name of the ruleset, its target type, enforcement level, and conditions under which the ruleset applies.

name: Update a team
description: Updates a team's settings (e.g., name, description, privacy, parent team) within a github organization, identified by its slug and organization name.

input_parameters.title: UpdateATeamRequest

org.title: Org
org.description: The organization's name (case-insensitive).

name.title: Name
name.description: New display name for the team.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description:
The desired privacy level for the team. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `privacy` intact. When a team is nested, the `privacy` for parent teams cannot be `secret`.
  - For a non-nested team:
    - `secret`: Visible only to organization owners and members of this team.
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
  - For a parent or child team:
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.

team_slug.title: Team Slug
team_slug.description: The team's unique slug identifier (typically URL-friendly version of team name).

permission.title: Permission
permission.description: **Deprecated**. Default permission (pull, push, or admin) for new repositories added to this team. This may be removed in future versions.

description.title: Description
examples: Responsible for all frontend development aspects of Project X
description.description: New team description; an empty string clears the existing description.

parent_team_id.title: Parent Team Id
parent_team_id.description: The unique identifier (ID) of an existing team to set as this team's parent. This establishes a nested team structure. To remove an existing parent team relationship and make this a top-level team, explicitly pass `null` for this field (e.g., by setting this parameter to Python `None`). If this parameter is not provided in the request, the current parent team, if any, remains unchanged.

notification_setting.title: Notification Setting
notification_setting.description:
The notification setting the team has chosen. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `notification_setting` intact.
  - `notifications_enabled`: Team members receive notifications when the team is @mentioned.
  - `notifications_disabled`: No one receives notifications for team @mentions.

input_parameters.description: Parameters to update a team's settings in a GitHub organization.

data.title: Data
data.description: The full, updated team object as returned by the GitHub API.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action allows users to update various settings for a specified team within a GitHub organization. 
    Users can modify the team's name, description, privacy level, notification settings, and parent team.

name: Update branch protection
description: Updates the protection settings for an existing branch in a repository, which must not contain wildcard characters.

input_parameters.title: UpdateBranchProtectionRequest

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

branch.title: Branch
branch.description: Name of the branch to protect; wildcard characters are not allowed (use GraphQL API for wildcard support).

lock_branch.title: Lock Branch
lock_branch.description: If `True`, sets the branch to read-only, preventing pushes.

enforce_admins.title: Enforce Admins
enforce_admins.description: Enforces configured restrictions for repository administrators. Set to `True` to enforce for admins, or `None`/`null` to disable.

allow_deletions.title: Allow Deletions
allow_deletions.description: Allows deletion of the protected branch.

block_creations.title: Block Creations
block_creations.description: If `True`, push restrictions also apply to creating matching new branches, unless pusher has explicit push access.

allow_force_pushes.title: Allow Force Pushes
allow_force_pushes.description: Permits force pushes to the protected branch. Set to `True` to allow, `None`/`null` to block.

allow_fork_syncing.title: Allow Fork Syncing
allow_fork_syncing.description: Allows users to pull upstream changes into their fork when the branch is locked.

restrictions__apps.title: Restrictions  Apps
restrictions__apps.description: App `slug`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_users` or `restrictions_teams`. Empty list removes all.

restrictions__teams.title: Restrictions  Teams
restrictions__teams.description: Team `slug`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_apps` or `restrictions_users`. Empty list removes all.

restrictions__users.title: Restrictions  Users
restrictions__users.description: User `login`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_apps` or `restrictions_teams`. Empty list removes all.

required_linear_history.title: Required Linear History
required_linear_history.description: Enforces linear commit history by preventing merge commits. Repository must allow squash or rebase merging.

required__status__checks__checks.title: Required  Status  Checks  Checks
required__status__checks__checks.description: List of status checks required to merge. Each check object needs a `context` string and optionally an `app_id` integer.

required__status__checks__strict.title: Required  Status  Checks  Strict
required__status__checks__strict.description: Require branches to be up to date with the base branch before merging; if `True`, merges are blocked if the head branch is behind the base branch.

required_conversation_resolution.title: Required Conversation Resolution
required_conversation_resolution.description: Requires all conversations on code to be resolved before merging a pull request.

required__status__checks__contexts.title: Required  Status  Checks  Contexts
required__status__checks__contexts.description: **Deprecated**: List of status check contexts required to merge. If set by a GitHub App, future checks must come from that app. Use `checks` for finer control.

required__pull__request__reviews__dismis.title: Required  Pull  Request  Reviews  Dismiss  Stale  Reviews
required__pull__request__reviews__dismis.description: If `True`, new commits dismiss previous review approvals on a pull request branch.

required__pull__request__reviews__dismis.title: Required  Pull  Request  Reviews  Dismissal  Restrictions  Apps
required__pull__request__reviews__dismis.description: App `slug`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

required__pull__request__reviews__requir.title: Required  Pull  Request  Reviews  Require  Code  Owner  Reviews
required__pull__request__reviews__requir.description: If `True`, pull requests affecting files with code owners must be reviewed by them.

required__pull__request__reviews__requir.title: Required  Pull  Request  Reviews  Require  Last  Push  Approval
required__pull__request__reviews__requir.description: If `True`, the most recent push must be approved by someone other than the pusher.

required__pull__request__reviews__dismis.title: Required  Pull  Request  Reviews  Dismissal  Restrictions  Teams
required__pull__request__reviews__dismis.description: Team `slug`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

required__pull__request__reviews__dismis.title: Required  Pull  Request  Reviews  Dismissal  Restrictions  Users
required__pull__request__reviews__dismis.description: User `login`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

required__pull__request__reviews__requir.title: Required  Pull  Request  Reviews  Required  Approving  Review  Count
required__pull__request__reviews__requir.description: Number of approving reviews (0-6) required to merge a pull request; 0 disables.

required__pull__request__reviews__bypass.title: Required  Pull  Request  Reviews  Bypass  Pull  Request  Allowances  Apps
required__pull__request__reviews__bypass.description: App `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes all.

required__pull__request__reviews__bypass.title: Required  Pull  Request  Reviews  Bypass  Pull  Request  Allowances  Teams
