name: Update git lfs preference
description: Sets the git large file storage (lfs) preference for a repository, typically before initiating a source import.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

use_lfs.title: Use Lfs
use_lfs.description: Specifies whether to store large files using Git LFS during the import. `opt_in` enables Git LFS, storing large files. `opt_out` disables Git LFS, removing large files during the import.

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary containing the details of the import project, reflecting the updated Git LFS preference and current import status. This includes information such as the VCS type, import URL, status, and LFS usage.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action updates the Git Large File Storage (LFS) preference for a repository during a source import.
    NOTE: This endpoint is DEPRECATED and no longer available as of April 12, 2024. GitHub has retired the Source Imports REST API.

name: Update information about a github pages site
description: Updates the configuration for a github pages site (e.g., custom domain, https, build type, source); requires github pages to be enabled for the repository, and if `build type` is 'workflow', a corresponding github actions workflow must be configured.

input_parameters.title: UpdateInformationAboutAGithubPagesSiteRequest

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

cname.title: Cname
cname.description: Specify a custom domain for the GitHub Pages site. Sending a `null` or empty string will remove the custom domain. For more information, see GitHub's documentation on custom domains for Pages.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

source.title: Source
source.description: The publishing source for the GitHub Pages site. Specifies the branch and directory from which the site is built. 'gh-pages' uses the gh-pages branch. 'master' uses the master (or main) branch. 'master /docs' uses the /docs folder on the master (or main) branch. This parameter is ignored if `build_type` is 'workflow'.

build_type.title: Build Type
build_type.description: The build process for the GitHub Pages site. Use 'workflow' if the site is built by a custom GitHub Actions workflow. Use 'legacy' if the site is built by GitHub when changes are pushed to a specific branch.

https_enforced.title: Https Enforced
https_enforced.description: Specify whether HTTPS should be enforced for the GitHub Pages site. `True` enforces HTTPS, `False` does not.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `UpdateInformationAboutAGithubPagesSite`

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates the configuration settings for a GitHub Pages site. It allows users to specify options such as custom domains, HTTPS enforcement, and the build type for the site.

deprecated.displayName: Update information about a github pages site

name: Update pull request review protection
description: Updates pull request review protection settings (e.g., required approvals, review dismissal, bypass allowances) for a branch; branch protection features must be available for the repository.

input_parameters.title: UpdatePullRequestReviewProtectionRequest

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

branch.title: Branch
branch.description: Branch name for protection rules (no wildcards; use GraphQL API for wildcards).

dismiss_stale_reviews.title: Dismiss Stale Reviews
dismiss_stale_reviews.description: If `true`, new commits dismiss previous approving reviews; `false` keeps approvals valid.

require_code_owner_reviews.title: Require Code Owner Reviews
require_code_owner_reviews.description: If `true`, pull requests affecting files with code owners require their review. See [about code owners](https://docs.github.com/articles/about-code-owners/).

require_last_push_approval.title: Require Last Push Approval
require_last_push_approval.description: If `true`, the most recent push requires approval by someone other than the pusher (GitHub Enterprise feature).

dismissal__restrictions__apps.title: Dismissal  Restrictions  Apps
dismissal__restrictions__apps.description: App `slug`s permitted to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes restrictions.

dismissal__restrictions__teams.title: Dismissal  Restrictions  Teams
dismissal__restrictions__teams.description: Team `slug`s permitted to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes restrictions.

dismissal__restrictions__users.title: Dismissal  Restrictions  Users
dismissal__restrictions__users.description: User `login`s permitted to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes restrictions.

required_approving_review_count.title: Required Approving Review Count
required_approving_review_count.description: Number of approving reviews (1-6) required for merging; 0 to disable.

bypass__pull__request__allowances__apps.title: Bypass  Pull  Request  Allowances  Apps
bypass__pull__request__allowances__apps.description: App `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes allowances.

bypass__pull__request__allowances__teams.title: Bypass  Pull  Request  Allowances  Teams
bypass__pull__request__allowances__teams.description: Team `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes allowances.

bypass__pull__request__allowances__users.title: Bypass  Pull  Request  Allowances  Users
bypass__pull__request__allowances__users.description: User `login`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes allowances.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for updating pull request review protection settings for a branch, including review requirements and bypass allowances.

data.title: Data
data.description: Dictionary representing the full updated branch protection rule, including pull request review settings.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates the pull request review protection settings for a specified branch in a repository. It allows you to configure review requirements, dismissal restrictions, and permissions for users, teams, and apps regarding pull request reviews.

name: Update repository preferences for check suites
description: Updates repository preferences for automatic check suite creation on code pushes, allowing configuration for specific github apps that must be installed on the repository with `checks:write` permission.

input_parameters.title: UpdateRepositoryPreferencesForCheckSuitesRequest

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., username or organization). The name is not case sensitive.

auto_trigger_checks.title: Auto Trigger Checks
auto_trigger_checks.description: A list of preference objects for configuring automatic CheckSuite creation for specific GitHub Apps. Each object in the list must contain an `app_id` (integer: the ID of the GitHub App) and a `setting` (boolean: `true` to enable automatic triggers for that app on code push, `false` to disable). If this field is omitted in the request, the repository's existing `auto_trigger_checks` preferences are not modified by this specific update action.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for the `UpdateRepositoryPreferencesForCheckSuites` action. Specifies the repository and the check suite preferences to update.

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary containing the results of the update operation. This typically includes a `preferences` object with the updated `auto_trigger_checks` array, and a `repository` object detailing the repository for which preferences were set.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates the preferences for automatic Check Suite creation in a specified repository. It allows configuration of preferences for particular GitHub Apps that are authorized on the repository.

deprecated.displayName: Update repository preferences for check suites

name: Update resource access with tokens
description: Revokes organization access for the personal access tokens identified by `pat ids`; this action must be performed by a github app, and `pat ids` must be valid and associated with the organization.

input_parameters.title: UpdateResourceAccessWithTokensRequest

org.title: Org
org.description: Organization name (case-insensitive).

action.title: Action
action.description: Operation to perform on the fine-grained personal access tokens, resulting in removal of their access.

pat_ids.title: Pat Ids
pat_ids.description: Fine-grained personal access token IDs for which access will be revoked.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `UpdateResourceAccessWithTokens`

data.title: Data
data.description: API response. May be empty on successful revocation (HTTP 204) or contain error diagnostics.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action revokes access for specified personal access tokens in a given organization. It ensures that the tokens no longer have permissions associated with the organization, bolstering security and control over token access.

    IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication, not personal access token authentication.

name: Update status check protection
description: Updates required status checks for a branch, optionally requiring it to be up-to-date before merging.

input_parameters.title: UpdateStatusCheckProtectionRequest

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

branch.title: Branch
branch.description: The name of the branch. Wildcard characters are not allowed. To use wildcard characters in branch names, please use the GraphQL API.

checks.title: Checks
checks.description: A list of status checks to require in order to merge into this branch. Each check object should have a `context` (string) and an optional `app_id` (integer). Example: `[{"context": "continuous-integration/jenkins", "app_id": 12345}]`

strict.title: Strict
strict.description: Specifies whether to require branches to be up to date before merging.

contexts.title: Contexts
contexts.description: **Deprecated**: A list of status checks to require in order to merge into this branch. If any of these checks were recently set by a particular GitHub App, they will be required to come from that app in the future for the branch to merge. For more fine-grained control, use `checks` instead of `contexts`.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `UpdateStatusCheckProtection`

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates the required status checks for a branch. It can enforce that branches must be up-to-date before they can be merged.

name: Update the authenticated user
description: Updates the authenticated user's github profile; a new public email must be verified, and existing private emails remain private even if specified.

input_parameters.title: UpdateTheAuthenticatedUserRequest

bio.title: Bio
bio.description: The new short biography for the user. Provide `null` to clear the existing bio.

blog.title: Blog
blog.description: The new URL for the user's blog. Provide `null` to clear the existing blog URL. For example, 'https://github.blog'.

name.title: Name
name.description: The new full name for the user. For example, 'Mona Lisa Octocat'.

email.title: Email
email.description: The user's new publicly visible email address. This email must be verified on the user's GitHub account. If the provided email is not verified or not set to public, the update for this field might be ignored.

company.title: Company
company.description: The new company affiliation for the user. Provide `null` to clear the existing company. For example, 'GitHub'.

hireable.title: Hireable
hireable.description: Indicates whether the user is available for hire. Set to `true` if available, or `false` if not.

location.title: Location
location.description: The new geographical location of the user. Provide `null` to clear the existing location. For example, 'San Francisco'.

twitter_username.title: Twitter Username
twitter_username.description: The user's new Twitter username (without the leading '@'). Provide `null` to clear the existing Twitter username. For example, 'github'.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for updating the authenticated user's profile information.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates the authenticated user's profile information on GitHub. It allows modifications to various attributes such as name, email, and biography.

name: Update token org access
description: Revokes a fine-grained personal access token's access to an organization, usable only by github apps when the token has existing access to that organization.

input_parameters.title: UpdateTokenOrgAccessRequest

org.title: Org
org.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

action.title: Action
action.description: Specifies the 'revoke' action, which removes the token's access to the specified organization.

pat_id.title: Pat Id
pat_id.description: The unique identifier (ID) of the fine-grained personal access token.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for revoking a fine-grained personal access token's access to an organization.

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary containing the response from the API. For a successful revocation (which typically returns an HTTP 204 No Content status), this dictionary is usually empty.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
Revokes a fine-grained personal access token's organization access.
    REQUIRES GitHub App authentication with 'Personal access tokens' org permissions (write).
    Personal access tokens cannot call this endpoint. Use LIST_ORG_RESOURCE_ACCESS_TOKENS
    to find valid token IDs.

name: Upload an analysis as sarif data
description: Uploads a gzipped and base64 encoded sarif file to a github repository for a specific commit and reference; use `checkout uri` if sarif paths are absolute.

input_parameters.title: UploadAnAnalysisAsSarifDataRequest

ref.title: Ref
ref.description: The full Git reference for the analysis, formatted as `refs/heads/<branch name>`, `refs/tags/<tag>`, `refs/pull/<number>/merge`, or `refs/pull/<number>/head`.

repo.title: Repo
repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This is case-insensitive.

sarif.title: Sarif
examples: eJzVst... (actual gzipped and Base64 encoded SARIF content)
sarif.description: A Base64 encoded string of a gzipped SARIF file. For more information, see '[SARIF support for code scanning](https://docs.github.com/code-security/secure-coding/sarif-support-for-code-scanning)'.

validate.title: Validate
validate.description: Specifies whether the uploaded SARIF file should be validated against the code scanning specifications. This is useful for integrators to ensure their SARIF files are correctly processed and rendered by GitHub.

tool_name.title: Tool Name
tool_name.description: Name of the tool that generated the analysis. GitHub defaults this to "API" if omitted. A tool GUID in the SARIF can be used for filtering alerts.

commit_sha.title: Commit Sha
commit_sha.description: The SHA of the commit to which the analysis you are uploading relates. Must be a 40-character Git SHA.

started_at.title: Started At
started_at.description: The timestamp indicating when the analysis run began, in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

checkout_uri.title: Checkout Uri
checkout_uri.description: The base URI of the checkout directory as it appears in the SARIF file. This property is used to convert absolute file paths to relative paths, enabling alerts to be correctly mapped to their location in the repository.

input_parameters.description: Request schema for `UploadAnAnalysisAsSarifData`

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary containing the response from the GitHub API. Upon successful upload, this typically includes an `id` for the SARIF analysis, which can be used to track its status or retrieve details later. It may also include an `url` to access the analysis.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action uploads the SARIF analysis file to a specific repository on GitHub. It allows for tracking and managing code security analysis results efficiently.

deprecated.displayName: Upload an analysis as sarif data
